name: Immune Rejuvenation Coverage Repair

scope.question: Which known source-completeness gaps for Immune Rejuvenation need repair, and does resolving them change the coverage verdict or public boundaries?

summary: Coverage repair staged mTOR/TORC1 source, study, finding, intervention, review, active-registry, and outlook-support-map records for Immune Rejuvenation. The staged outlook keeps evidence_stage human_functional_benefit because older-adult trials include vaccine-response, infection-rate, and respiratory-illness endpoints, but changes momentum to mixed and confidence to low because RTB101 had a phase 3 miss, spermidine and TRIIM are small or biomarker-heavy, and active direct trials remain no-results.

materiality_decision.rationale: The repair materially improves source support, limiting-evidence, registry, recent-review, and taxonomy-boundary coverage. It also changes the public read from moderate/steady to low/mixed without broadening the claim beyond narrowly supported human immune-function endpoints.

search_log.summary: Confirmed primary mTOR/TORC1 older-adult trial abstracts, the existing 2026 spermidine vaccine-response pilot, the 2026 immunosenescence/vaccine/mTOR/microbiota review, and adjacent vaccine or telomerase context. PMID 25540326, PMID 29997249, PMID 33977284, and PMID 42169620 were staged; PMID 42169618 was reused from the live public layer; PMID 42228420 and PMID 37086366 were retained as context-only or adjacent-track material.

search_log.query: NCT07363057, NCT04375657, NCT04139915, broad immune-aging and mTOR older-adult trial searches
search_log.summary: Confirmed GIANTS-1 as a recruiting phase 2a immune-aging biomarker trial with no posted results, TRIIM-X as still recruiting with no posted results and last update posted May 7, 2025, and NCT04139915 as a withdrawn RTB101 follow-up after the phase 3 miss.

search_log.source_name: Local public records and coverage assessment
search_log.query: Immune Rejuvenation outlook, TRIIM, spermidine, TA-65, sirolimus, prior bundle, and June 11 coverage artifacts
search_log.summary: Compared the current moderate/steady public outlook and thin coverage assessment against staged mTOR, review, registry, TRIIM, and spermidine support. The repair prioritizes a support map and new missing records rather than rewriting existing autophagy, epigenetic, telomerase, or rapalog records.

trial_watch_checks.summary: Staged as direct immune-aging recruiting no-results context because it measures NK cells, CD8 T cells, safety, physical function, cognition, microbiome, and health-event endpoints.
Reused as existing no-results TRIIM-X trial-watch context; the current registry view still showed recruiting status and no posted results.

Excluded from staging because the registry had zero enrollment and was withdrawn after the related phase 3 RTB101 trial missed its primary endpoint; the staged Lancet Healthy Longevity paper captures the material limiting evidence.

excluded_sources.title: Age-related differences in immune responses to inactivated influenza and adjuvanted recombinant herpes zoster vaccines
excluded_sources.reason: Useful vaccine-response and inflammaging benchmark, but it tests vaccine products and age-response differences rather than an immune-rejuvenation intervention.

excluded_sources.title: Activation of telomerase by TA-65 enhances immunity and reduces inflammation post myocardial infarction
excluded_sources.reason: Already represented as telomerase-restoration disease-context evidence. It is adjacent immune biomarker context, not a core Immune Rejuvenation support-map anchor.

excluded_sources.title: Effect of RTB101 on Illness Associated With Respiratory Tract Infections in Elderly Adults
excluded_sources.reason: Withdrawn with zero enrollment after the phase 3 RTB101 miss; material program risk is captured by PMID 33977284.

excluded_sources.title: Sirolimus in Older Adults
excluded_sources.reason: Already represented in the rapalog/nutrient-sensing layer as a small no-results older-adult sirolimus registry; not added to this repair because it is not immune-endpoint-specific.

notes: The staged repair intentionally keeps the evidence stage at human_functional_benefit, but narrows confidence and momentum to low and mixed.
The mTOR/TORC1 records are included because they have clinically legible older-adult immune-function endpoints; they are also explicitly bounded as rapalog/nutrient-sensing evidence.
Existing TRIIM and spermidine records are referenced in the outlook support map rather than restaged, preserving their current ownership in epigenetic/neuroendocrine and autophagy layers.
Month-only ClinicalTrials.gov completion dates were normalized to the first day of the month in structured date fields and preserved as month-level timing in text notes.
next_actions: Run source-fidelity, interpretation/forecast, safety-limitations, and taxonomy-mapping review for the staged repair bundle.
If approved and published, return Immune Rejuvenation to ordinary surveillance with watch items for GIANTS-1, TRIIM-X, and any renewed mTOR/TORC1 immune-function programs.
